chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 1516 of 2025 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.9251/2025. 2. For orders on office objection No.22. 3. For orders on CMA No.7614/2025. 4. For orders on CMA No.7615/2025. 5. For hearing of main case. 13.05.2025 Mr. Muhammad Ramzan Panhwar, advocate for the petitioner. 1. 1. Urgency granted. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exceptions. 2, 4 & 5. This petition was heard on 21.04.2025, however, upon being confronted as to maintainability, time was sought to prepare. Today, once again, learned counsel remains unable to satisfy the court on account of maintainability. Per learned counsel, no GD has been filed by the petitioner as of date since the consignment has not yet arrived, however, this petition has been preferred seeking to revive some exemption admittedly withdrawn / no longer holding the field. Counsel demonstrated the existence of previous instruments, issued in 2006 and thereafter, wherein the present quantum of levy was different, however, remained unable to assist as to how the prevailing duties, taxes etc. could be disregarded simply because the quantum had been lesser earlier. Prima facie the nature of instrument and manner of imposition was consistent in all the SROs demonstrated before us. Learned counsel remained unable to assist as to how an instrument raising the quantum of levy was considered discrepant, when identical instruments, reducing the quantum, were being relied upon. In view hereof and coupled with the ratio illumined recently by the Supreme Court in Mekotex1, no case is made out to merit invocation of the discretionary2 writ jurisdiction of this Court, therefore, this petition, along with pending application/s, is hereby dismissed in limine.

Judge Judge 1 Per Mansoor Ali Shah J in CIR vs. Mekotex (CPLA 824-K of 2023) & connected matters; yet unreported judgment dated 03.07.2024. 2 Syed Iqbal Hussain Shah Gillani vs. PBC & Others reported as 2021 SCMR 425; Muhammad Fiaz Khan vs. Ajmer Khan & Another reported as 2010 SCMR 105.
